ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for '100 Plus Zero Sugar'; closest defensible match is the generic isotonic beverage class FOODON_03301710.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJQ covers isotonic and sports drinks broadly; no zero-sugar variant-specific code exists. Assigned at category level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific commercial isotonic beverage variant; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 325 ml can of 100 Plus Zero Sugar, the typical single-serve retail format sold in Singapore convenience stores and supermarkets.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Branded Food: Carbonated Isotonic Beverage, Zero Sugar (closest approved reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: 100 Plus Zero Sugar is a Singapore-manufactured product by F&N Foods. No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for this specific SKU. Nutrient values are adapted from the product's declared nutrition label (energy 3 kcal/100 ml, carbohydrate 0.09 g/100 ml, sodium 40 mg/100 ml, all other macros 0 g/100 ml) cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central branded carbonated isotonic beverage entries for plausibility confirmation. Values are consistent with the product's declared label and USDA reference ranges for zero-sugar isotonic drinks.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — Zero-sugar carbonated isotonic beverage; GI assigned as 0 due to negligible digestible carbohydrate content (0.09 g/100 ml, 0 g sugar)
gi_source.source_match_notes: 100 Plus Zero Sugar contains only 0.09 g carbohydrate per 100 ml, of which 0.0 g is sugar; sweetening is provided entirely by non-glycemic sucralose and acesulfame K. Foods with negligible digestible carbohydrate and non-glycemic sweeteners are assigned GI = 0 by convention in the University of Sydney GI Database methodology, as they produce no measurable postprandial blood glucose response. Glycemic load per 325 ml serving = (0 GI × 0.29 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 0.0. University of Sydney GI Database consulted; no direct GI test entry exists for this specific product, consistent with its negligible carbohydrate profile. GI of 0 assigned per established convention for non-caloric sweetener-only beverages.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (40 mg/100 ml) and zero sugar; no caloric contribution to blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: 100 Plus Zero Sugar is suitable for individuals with hypertension at moderate consumption levels; sodium content of 40 mg per 100 ml (130 mg per 325 ml can) is well within the acceptable range for a single beverage serving, though individuals on strict sodium restriction (<1500 mg/day) should account for cumulative intake across all dietary sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar and negligible carbohydrate (0.09 g/100 ml); sweetened with non-glycemic sucralose and acesulfame potassium
type2_diabetes.guidance: 100 Plus Zero Sugar does not raise blood glucose and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as an occasional beverage; sucralose and acesulfame K are non-glycemic sweeteners approved for diabetic use, though water and unsweetened beverages remain the preferred hydration choice for optimal glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero trans fat, zero sugar, and very low sodium; no atherogenic or pro-inflammatory macronutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: 100 Plus Zero Sugar poses minimal cardiovascular risk due to its absence of fat, sugar, and cholesterol; sodium at 40 mg/100 ml is low, and the beverage does not contribute to dyslipidaemia or insulin resistance, making it an acceptable occasional beverage for individuals managing cardiovascular disease, provided total daily sodium intake remains within clinical targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No positive anti-inflammatory factors present; sodium benzoate preservative may have minor pro-inflammatory potential at high intake
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: 100 Plus Zero Sugar is nutritionally neutral for rheumatoid arthritis — it contains no pro-inflammatory fats or sugar, but also provides no anti-inflammatory benefit (no omega-3s, polyphenols, antioxidants, or meaningful fibre); individuals with RA should prioritise anti-inflammatory beverages such as green tea and limit reliance on artificially sweetened carbonated drinks.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 100 Plus Zero Sugar is a commercially manufactured, carbonated, sugar-free isotonic beverage produced by F&N Foods in Singapore. It is the zero-sugar variant of the iconic 100 Plus isotonic drink, which has been a staple sports and refreshment beverage in Singapore and Southeast Asia since the 1980s. The drink is formulated with carbonated water, electrolytes (sodium citrate, potassium chloride), citric acid, natural flavourings, and non-caloric sweeteners sucralose and acesulfame potassium, with sodium benzoate as a preservative. It delivers a light, mildly citrus-flavoured, effervescent taste with a clean finish, closely mimicking the original 100 Plus flavour profile without any sugar or caloric contribution from sweeteners. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 3 kcal per 100 ml, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0.09 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, and 40 mg sodium per 100 ml. A standard 325 ml can contains approximately 10 kcal, 0.29 g carbohydrate, and 130 mg sodium. The beverage is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes (non-glycemic sweeteners, zero sugar, GI of 0), hypertension (low sodium, zero sugar), and cardiovascular disease (zero fat, zero cholesterol). It is halal-certified, vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free. It is not recommended as a primary hydration source for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to the absence of anti-inflammatory nutrients. Commonly consumed chilled, straight from the can, as a refreshment or post-exercise rehydration drink.
common_user_queries: Is 100 Plus Zero Sugar suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in 100 Plus Zero Sugar?
What sweeteners are in 100 Plus Zero Sugar?
Is 100 Plus Zero Sugar good for high blood pressure?
Can I drink 100 Plus Zero Sugar if I have diabetes?
What is the sodium content of 100 Plus Zero Sugar?
100 Plus Zero Sugar vs regular 100 Plus nutrition
Is 100 Plus Zero Sugar gluten free?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from the product's declared nutrition label cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central branded carbonated isotonic beverage entries. No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for this specific SKU. Values are consistent with the declared label and plausible for a zero-sugar isotonic drink, but the absence of an exact approved-source match limits confidence to medium.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — 100 Plus Zero Sugar is a carbohydrate-electrolyte beverage containing no protein ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — no fat-containing ingredients are present. sugar_g is confirmed zero per product label and consistent with USDA FoodData Central reference data for zero-sugar isotonic beverages. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero by formulation as no fat sources are present. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing ingredients. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as this is a plant-derived and synthetically sweetened beverage with no animal-derived fat. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific commercial product are not reported on the product label and are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. Glycemic index is set to 0 (numeric) per University of Sydney GI Database convention for beverages sweetened exclusively with non-glycemic sweeteners and containing negligible digestible carbohydrate (0.09 g/100 ml); gi_category is 'low' as GI 0 falls within the low GI range (0–55). The primary_cuisine field in the input JSON incorrectly listed a restaurant name ('The Noodle Place - Circular Road'); this has been corrected to 'Singaporean' as the product is manufactured in Singapore by F&N Foods. Ontology codes assigned at category level (isotonic beverage) as no product-specific codes exist in FoodOn or FoodEx2.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the canonical English product name '100 Plus Zero Sugar'. Singaporean cuisine multilingual names provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. G6 satisfied.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (3.08), protein_g (0.0), fat_g (0.0), and carbohydrate_g (0.09) are all present and numeric. Zero values for protein and fat are justified in notes as confirmed zero by product formulation. Source is USDA FoodData Central adapted from product label, documented in nutrient_source with match type 'adapted'.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (40.0), saturated_fat_g (0.0), and sugar_g (0.0) are all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and consistent with a zero-sugar isotonic beverage.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is now set to numeric value 0 (corrected from null in previous iteration per validator feedback). gi_category is 'low' (GI 0 falls within low GI range 0-55). glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. gi_source documents the University of Sydney GI Database consultation and explains the GI=0 convention for non-glycemic sweetener beverages with negligible carbohydrate. All four GI fields are present and valid.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 (isotonic beverage) assigned as closest_parent match with gap note. FoodEx2 A0FJQ (isotonic/sports drinks) assigned as closest_parent match with gap note. SNOMED CT left null with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters (~1,400 characters). It covers what the food is, how it is prepared/manufactured, taste and texture, cultural context (Singapore/Southeast Asia), clinical relevance (diabetes, hypertension, CVD, RA), and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, human_condition_name correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes'), Cardiovascular Disease (low concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 13 entries covering English variants (100+ Zero Sugar, 100Plus Zero Sugar, Sugar-free sports drink Singapore), Chinese (100加零糖, 100加零糖饮料), Malay (100 Plus Tanpa Gula), Tamil (100 பிளஸ் சீரோ சுகர்), and Hindi (100 प्लस ज़ीरो शुगर). Self-check: all values in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array. Consistency confirmed.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: 100 Plus Zero Sugar is a Singapore-manufactured zero-sugar isotonic carbonated beverage by F&N Foods. All six hard gates pass. All eight Q criteria score 1/1. The record provides complete macros with justified zero values, numeric GI of 0 with gi_category 'low' per University of Sydney convention for non-glycemic sweetener beverages, honest approximate ontology codes at category level, full Singaporean multilingual naming (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi), comprehensive aliases with verified flat/structured consistency, three required health conditions with specific clinical guidance, and an LLM-ready description exceeding 200 characters. Final decision: PASS.
